evals.name: scaffold-toml
evals.prompt: I'm starting a small Go library for parsing and formatting TOML files. Scaffold a new package called `toml` so I can start filling in the format details.
evals.expected_output: A scaffolded Go package at ./toml/ with doc.go, tokenizer.go, tokenizer_test.go, parser.go, parser_test.go, printer.go, printer_test.go, and CLAUDE.md. `go build` and `go test` should both succeed.

assertions.text: go test ./toml/... exits cleanly with all tests passing
tokenizer.go defines `func Tokenize(r io.Reader) iter.Seq2[Token, error]`
printer.go defines `func Print(w io.Writer, f *File) error`
tokenizer.go defines `tokenizerAction` type with signature `func(t *tokenizer, yield func(Token, error) bool) tokenizerAction`
parser.go defines a generic `parserAction[T any]` type with signature `func(p *parser, t T) (parserAction[T], error)`
printer.go defines `printerAction` type with signature `func(pr *printer, f *File) printerAction`
test files call t.Parallel() at both function and subtest level
parser_test.go drives the public Parse() function (does not construct AST nodes by hand)
printer_test.go contains a round-trip test (TestPrinterRoundTrip or equivalent) showing the Parse→Print→Parse pattern
toml/CLAUDE.md documents the action-loop pattern, the inner-action-loop rule for complex types, and the testing conventions

evals.name: scaffold-ini
evals.prompt: scaffold a new go text file library package for the ini format. call it ini.
evals.expected_output: A scaffolded Go package at ./ini/ with all 8 files. `go build` and `go test` should both succeed.

assertions.text: go test ./ini/... exits cleanly with all tests passing
tokenizer.go defines `func Tokenize(r io.Reader) iter.Seq2[Token, error]`
printer.go defines `func Print(w io.Writer, f *File) error`
tokenizer.go defines `tokenizerAction` type with signature `func(t *tokenizer, yield func(Token, error) bool) tokenizerAction`

parser_test.go drives the public Parse() function (does not construct AST nodes by hand)
printer_test.go contains a round-trip test (TestPrinterRoundTrip or equivalent)
ini/CLAUDE.md documents the action-loop pattern, the inner-action-loop rule for complex types, and the testing conventions

evals.name: scaffold-graphql
evals.prompt: I need to start a new Go package called `graphql` for working with GraphQL schema documents. I'll be using the implement-go-text-file-library agent afterward to fill in the real grammar from the spec, but I want the boilerplate in place now.
evals.expected_output: A scaffolded Go package at ./graphql/ with all 8 files. `go build` and `go test` should both succeed.

assertions.text: go test ./graphql/... exits cleanly with all tests passing
tokenizer.go defines `func Tokenize(r io.Reader) iter.Seq2[Token, error]`

graphql/CLAUDE.md documents the action-loop pattern, the inner-action-loop rule for complex types, and the testing conventions
